={main}> {/* Logo */}
{/* Content */}
{greeting} Thanks for signing up — we're glad to have an educator on the list. We're building Agathon to give teachers real superpowers. Not more paperwork, not another dashboard to check — actual insight into how your students think and where they get stuck. Our AI doesn't replace you. It gives you the visibility you've always wanted. What you'll get: — Live insight into student thinking as they work through problems — AI-generated feedback you can review and send in seconds, not hours — Google Classroom integration so it fits into your existing workflow

}>— Early access with free credits when we launch What's next: — We'll reach out when your spot is ready — Teachers get priority access — you're at the front of the line — We'll share what we're building and ask for your input along the way Hit reply if you want to tell us about your classroom — what subjects you teach, what grade levels, what drives you crazy. We actually read every reply, and it directly shapes what we build. {referralLink && ( <>

. {/* Logo */}

{greeting} Thanks for signing up — we're glad

={main}> {/* Logo */}
{/* Content */}
{greeting} Thanks for signing up — it means a lot that you're looking out for your child's learning. We're building Agathon because we think AI can do something better than just spit out answers. It can actually help kids understand the why behind problems — building real confidence, not just grades. Think of it as a patient, always-available tutor that adapts to how your child thinks. How Agathon helps your child: — Guides them through problems step by step instead of giving away answers — Adapts to their learning style and pace — no one-size-fits-all — Works across math and science, from arithmetic to calculus — Built to be safe, focused, and distraction-free What's next: — We'll reach out when your family's spot is ready — Early families get free credits to try everything out — We'll keep you updated as we add new subjects and features Hit reply if you want to tell us about your child — what grade they're in, what subjects they find tricky, what you wish existed for them. We actually read every reply. {referralLink && ( <>
